sentences.chinese: 卷六十六 公孫劉田王楊蔡陳鄭傳第三十六
sentences.literal: Volume 66: Biographies of Gongsun, Liu, Tian, Wang, Yang, Cai, Chen, Zheng, the Thirty-Sixth.
sentences.idiomatic: Volume 66: Biographies of Gongsun, Liu, Tian, Wang, Yang, Cai, Chen, and Zheng—the thirty-sixth series.

sentences.chinese: 公孫賀
sentences.literal: Gongsun He.
sentences.idiomatic: Gongsun He

sentences.chinese: 公孫賀字子叔，北地義渠人也。
sentences.literal: Gongsun He, courtesy name Zishu, was a man of Yiqu in Beidi.
sentences.idiomatic: Gongsun He, whose courtesy name was Zishu, came from Yiqu in Beidi commandery.

sentences.chinese: 賀祖父昆邪，景帝時為隴西守，以將軍擊吳、楚有功，封平曲侯，著書十餘篇。
sentences.literal: He's grandfather Kunxie, in Emperor Jing's time was Guardian of Longxi; as a general he struck Wu and Chu with merit, was enfeoffed Marquis of Pingqu, and wrote more than ten treatises.
sentences.idiomatic: His grandfather Kunxie had served as Guardian of Longxi under Emperor Jing; as a general he campaigned against Wu and Chu with distinction, was enfeoffed as Marquis of Pingqu, and left behind more than ten written works.

sentences.chinese: 賀子敬聲，代賀為太僕，父子並居公卿位。
sentences.literal: He's son Jingsheng replaced He as Grand Coachman; father and son together held ministerial rank.
sentences.idiomatic: His son Jingsheng succeeded him as Grand Coachman, so father and son both stood among the highest ministers of state.

sentences.chinese: 敬聲以皇后姊子，驕奢不奉法，征和中擅用北軍錢千九百萬，發覺，下獄。
sentences.literal: Jingsheng, as the empress's sister's son, was arrogant and extravagant and did not obey the law; in the Zhenghe era he misappropriated nineteen million cash of the Northern Army's funds; when this was discovered, he was cast into prison.
sentences.idiomatic: Jingsheng was the empress's nephew by her sister. He lived in swaggering luxury and flouted the statutes, and during the Zhenghe period he diverted nineteen million cash from the Northern Army's treasury. When the embezzlement came to light, he was thrown into jail.

sentences.chinese: 是時，詔捕陽陵硃安世不能得，上求之急，賀自請逐捕安世以贖敬聲罪。
sentences.literal: At that time an edict had ordered the arrest of Zhu Anshi of Yangling, who could not be taken; the emperor pressed the search urgently, and He himself requested to hunt down and capture Anshi in order to redeem Jingsheng's crime.
sentences.idiomatic: The court had long sought Zhu Anshi of Yangling without success, and the emperor was demanding results. Gongsun He volunteered to run Anshi to earth if that would redeem his son.

sentences.chinese: 上許之。
sentences.literal: The emperor granted it.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor agreed.

sentences.chinese: 後果得安世。
sentences.literal: Later they indeed obtained Anshi.
sentences.idiomatic: They did in fact seize Anshi.

sentences.chinese: 安世者，京師大俠也，聞賀欲以贖子，笑曰：「丞相禍及宗矣。
sentences.literal: Anshi was a great bravo of the capital; hearing that He wished to use this to ransom his son, he laughed and said, "The chancellor's calamity will reach his clan.
sentences.idiomatic: Anshi was one of the capital's most feared swordsmen. When he learned Gongsun He meant to buy his son's freedom with Anshi's capture, he laughed and said, "The chancellor has doomed his whole lineage.

sentences.chinese: 南山之行不足受我辭，斜谷之木不足為我械。」
sentences.literal: The paths of Mount Zhongnan are not enough to hold my deposition; the trees of the Xie Valley are not enough to make my fetters."
sentences.idiomatic: Not every trail on Mount Zhongnan could hold the confession I would give; not every tree in Xie Gorge could furnish stocks for me."

sentences.chinese: 安世遂從獄中上書，告敬聲與陽石公主私通，及使人巫祭祠詛上，且上甘泉當馳道埋偶人，祝詛有惡言。
sentences.literal: Anshi then from prison submitted a memorial accusing Jingsheng of illicit relations with Princess Yangshi, and of having men practice witchcraft, offer sacrifices, and curse the sovereign, and moreover that when the emperor went to Ganquan he would bury puppets on the post road and uttered malign curses in his invocations.
sentences.idiomatic: From his cell Anshi memorialized the throne, charging Jingsheng with adultery with Princess Yangshi, with hiring witches to sacrifice and curse the emperor, and with burying straw effigies along the imperial carriage road to Ganquan while uttering venomous imprecations.

sentences.chinese: 下有司案驗賀，窮治所犯，遂父子死獄中，家族。
sentences.literal: It was handed to the authorities to investigate He; they exhaustively prosecuted his offenses, and in the end father and son died in prison, and the clan was exterminated.
sentences.idiomatic: The case was referred to the judicial officials, who pursued every charge against Gongsun He. Father and son perished in prison, and their kin were extirpated to the last.

sentences.chinese: 巫蠱之禍起自硃安世，成於江充，遂及公主、皇后、太子，皆敗。
sentences.literal: The witchcraft disaster arose from Zhu Anshi, was completed through Jiang Chong, and thus reached the princesses, the empress, and the heir apparent—all were ruined.
sentences.idiomatic: The witchcraft terror began with Zhu Anshi and was brought to a head by Jiang Chong; in its wake the princesses, the empress, and the crown prince all fell.

sentences.chinese: 語在《江充》、《戾園傳》。
sentences.literal: The account is given in the biographies of Jiang Chong and the Liyuan Heir.
sentences.idiomatic: The full story appears in the biographies of Jiang Chong and the Liyuan heir.

sentences.chinese: 劉屈氂
sentences.literal: Liu Qu 氂
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Qu 氂

sentences.chinese: 劉屈氂，武帝庶兄中山靖王子也，不知其始所以進。
sentences.literal: Liu Qu 氂 was a son by a concubine of Emperor Wu's elder brother, the Prince Jing of Zhongshan; it is not known by what path he first advanced.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Qu 氂 was a bastard son of Emperor Wu's half-brother, the Prince Jing of Zhongshan; how he first came to court is not recorded.

sentences.chinese: 征和二年春，制詔御史：「故丞相賀倚舊故乘高勢而為邪，興美田以利子弟賓客，不顧元元，無益邊谷，貨賂上流，朕忍之久矣。
sentences.literal: In the second year of Zhenghe, spring, an edict to the Imperial Secretary read: "The former Chancellor He relied on old ties and high position to do wrong; he seized fine fields to profit sons, younger kin, and retainers; he had no regard for the common people, brought no benefit to the frontier granaries, and floated bribes upstream—I have endured this long.
sentences.idiomatic: In the spring of the second Zhenghe year the throne addressed the Imperial Secretary: "The late Chancellor Gongsun He traded on long acquaintance and lofty rank to act corruptly, seizing prime farmland for the benefit of his sons, clients, and guests. He showed no care for the populace, did nothing to fill the border granaries, and sent illicit gifts up the chain of command. I have swallowed these offenses for years.

sentences.chinese: 終不自革，乃以邊為援，使內郡自省作車，又令耕者自轉，以困農煩擾畜者，重馬傷枆，武備衰減；
sentences.literal: In the end he did not reform himself; then he used the frontier as a pretext, making the inner commanderies each build carts at their own expense, and ordering cultivators to haul their own grain, thereby harassing farmers, troubling those who raise livestock, injuring horses and wearing out draft animals, so that military readiness declined;
sentences.idiomatic: He never mended his ways. He used the border as an excuse to force interior commanderies to build wagons at their own cost and conscripted farmers to haul their own grain, vexing tillers and herdsmen alike, ruining horses and oxen, and sapping the army's strength;

sentences.chinese: 下吏妄賦，百姓流亡；
sentences.literal: inferior officials recklessly levied taxes, and the hundred surnames fled in exile;
sentences.idiomatic: petty officers plundered the people with arbitrary levies until commoners scattered as refugees;

sentences.chinese: 又詐為詔書，以奸傳硃安世。
sentences.literal: again he forged an edict in order treacherously to deliver Zhu Anshi.
sentences.idiomatic: he even forged an imperial rescript to entrap Zhu Anshi by false pretenses.

sentences.chinese: 獄已正於理。
sentences.literal: The prison case has been judged according to principle.
sentences.idiomatic: His guilt has been fixed by law.

sentences.chinese: 其以涿郡太守屈氂為左丞相，分丞相長史為兩府，以待天下遠方之選。
sentences.literal: Let the Guardian of Zhuo commandery, Qu 氂, be made Left Chancellor; divide the chief clerk of the chancellor's office into two bureaus, to await selection from all under Heaven in distant regions.
sentences.idiomatic: Therefore Qu 氂, Guardian of Zhuo, is appointed Left Chancellor; the chief clerk's office is split into two establishments so that talent may be drawn from every quarter of the realm.

sentences.chinese: 夫親親任賢，周、唐之道也。
sentences.literal: To love kin and employ the worthy is the way of Zhou and Tang.
sentences.idiomatic: Cherishing kinsmen and elevating the worthy—that was the way of the Zhou kings and of Tang of Shang.

sentences.chinese: 以澎戶二千二百封左丞相為澎侯。」
sentences.literal: With two thousand two hundred households of Peng, enfeoff the Left Chancellor as Marquis of Peng."
sentences.idiomatic: Invest the Left Chancellor with two thousand two hundred households and enfeoff him as Marquis of Peng."

sentences.chinese: 其秋，戾太子為江充所譖，殺充，發兵入丞相府，屈氂挺身逃，亡其印綬。
sentences.literal: That autumn the Liyang Heir-Apparent, slandered by Jiang Chong, killed Chong and raised troops into the chancellor's residence; Qu 氂 slipped away in flight and lost his seals and ribbons.
sentences.idiomatic: That autumn the crown prince Liu Ju, framed by Jiang Chong, executed Chong and marched on the chancellor's yamen. Qu 氂 fled headlong and left his official seals behind.

sentences.chinese: 是時，上避暑在甘泉宮，丞相長史乘疾置以聞。
sentences.literal: At that time the emperor was summering at Ganquan Palace; the chief clerk of the chancellor rode the express relay to report.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor was then at his Ganquan summer palace; the chancellor's chief clerk took the post relays to bring word.

sentences.chinese: 上問：「丞相何為？」
sentences.literal: The emperor asked, "What is the chancellor doing?"
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor asked what the chancellor was doing.

sentences.chinese: 對曰：「丞相秘之，未敢發兵。」
sentences.literal: He replied, "The chancellor has kept it secret and dared not raise troops."
sentences.idiomatic: The man answered that the chancellor had concealed the matter and had not yet dared to mobilize troops.

sentences.chinese: 上怒曰：「事籍籍如此，何謂秘也？
sentences.literal: The emperor angrily said, "The affair is notorious everywhere—what do you mean by secret?
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor flared: "The whole capital is buzzing—what secrecy is there?

sentences.chinese: 丞相無周公之風矣。
sentences.literal: The chancellor no longer has the manner of the Duke of Zhou.
sentences.idiomatic: The chancellor is no Duke of Zhou.

sentences.chinese: 周公不誅管、蔡乎？」
sentences.literal: Did the Duke of Zhou not execute Guan and Cai?"
sentences.idiomatic: Did not the Duke of Zhou put Guan and Cai to the sword?"

sentences.chinese: 乃賜丞相璽書曰：「捕斬反者，自有賞罰。
sentences.literal: Then he bestowed on the chancellor an edict written on jade: "Those who capture and behead rebels shall receive rewards and punishments as prescribed.
sentences.idiomatic: He then sent the chancellor a rescript on the jade tablet: "Whoever captures or slays the rebels will be rewarded or punished by regulation.

sentences.chinese: 以牛車為櫓，毋接短兵，多殺傷士眾。
sentences.literal: Use ox-carts as shields; do not join battle at short range, lest you kill and wound many soldiers and crowds.
sentences.idiomatic: Fight from behind ox-drawn barricades; do not close to hand strokes, or you will slaughter your own men by the thousands.

sentences.chinese: 堅閉城門，毋令反者得出。」
sentences.literal: Hold the city gates fast; do not let the rebels out."
sentences.idiomatic: Seal the gates; let no rebel escape."

sentences.chinese: 太子既誅充發兵，宣言帝在甘泉病困，疑有變，奸臣欲作亂。
sentences.literal: After the heir had killed Chong and raised troops, he declared that the emperor lay ill and imperiled at Ganquan, that he suspected a coup, and that crafty ministers meant to rebel.
sentences.idiomatic: Once the crown prince had executed Jiang Chong and called out the guard, he proclaimed that the emperor was gravely ill at Ganquan, that turncoats were plotting a coup, and that the dynasty was in danger.

sentences.chinese: 上於是從甘泉來，幸城西建章宮，詔發三輔近縣兵，部中二千石以下，丞相兼將。
sentences.literal: Thereupon the emperor came from Ganquan, favored Jianzhang Palace west of the city, and ordered the troops of the near counties of the Three Assistants to be raised; those within the commandery ranked at two thousand piculs and below, with the chancellor commanding jointly.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor rode down from Ganquan and took up quarters in Jianzhang Palace west of Chang'an, ordering the Three Assistants to mobilize neighboring counties. Every official below two thousand piculs fell under the chancellor's joint command.

sentences.chinese: 太子亦遣使者撟制赦長安中都官囚徒，發武庫兵，命少傅石德及賓客張光等分將，使長安囚如侯持節發長水及宣曲胡騎，皆以裝會。
sentences.literal: The heir also sent agents to counterfeit an edict pardoning convicts held by the capital offices, issued arms from the arsenal, appointed Junior Tutor Shi De and retainers such as Zhang Guang as separate commanders, and sent the Chang'an convict Ruhou with staff of authority to call out the Hu cavalry of Changshui and Xuqu—all to assemble in full kit.
sentences.idiomatic: The crown prince likewise forged a general amnesty for prisoners in the capital jails, opened the imperial armory, named his junior tutor Shi De and clients such as Zhang Guang as field commanders, and dispatched the convict Ruhou with a tallies to mobilize the Xiongnu auxiliaries of Changshui and Xuqu, ordering every detachment to muster under arms.

sentences.chinese: 侍郎莽通使長安，因追捕如侯，告胡人曰：「節有詐，勿聽也。」
sentences.literal: Gentleman for Court Audience Mang Tong was sent to Chang'an; he thereupon pursued and seized Ruhou, told the Hu troops, "The staff is forged—do not obey it,"
sentences.idiomatic: The Gentleman for Court Audience Mang Tong reached Chang'an, ran Ruhou down, and warned the Hu horsemen that the tallies were counterfeit and must be ignored,

sentences.chinese: 遂斬如侯，引騎入長安，又發輯濯士，以予大鴻臚商丘城。
sentences.literal: then beheaded Ruhou, led the cavalry into Chang'an, and also called out the oarsmen of the imperial barges, assigning them to the Grand Herald Shangqiu Cheng.
sentences.idiomatic: then executed Ruhou, rode into the city at the head of his column, impressed the palace boatmen, and placed them under Grand Herald Shangqiu Cheng.

sentences.chinese: 初，漢節純赤，以太子持赤節，故更為黃旄加上以相別。
sentences.literal: Formerly Han tallies had been pure cinnabar red; because the heir bore a red tally, they were changed to yellow yak-tail streamers added on top to distinguish them.
sentences.idiomatic: Han credentials had once been solid vermilion; because the crown prince carried a crimson staff, the court reissued tallies marked with yellow yak plumes so friend and foe could tell them apart.

sentences.chinese: 太子召監北軍使者任安發北軍兵，安受節已，閉軍門，不肯應太子。
sentences.literal: The heir summoned Ren An, the commissioner of the Northern Army, to raise the Northern Army; An received the staff, then shut the camp gates and would not answer the heir.
sentences.idiomatic: The crown prince summoned Ren An, overseer of the Northern Army, to turn out its garrison. Ren accepted the tally, then locked the gates and refused the prince.

sentences.chinese: 太子引兵去，驅四市人凡數萬眾，至長樂西闕下，逢丞相軍，合戰五日，死者數萬人，血流入溝中。
sentences.literal: The heir led his troops away, drove the people of the four markets—several tens of thousands in all—to the west gate of Weiyang Palace, met the chancellor's army, and fought for five days; several tens of thousands died, and blood flowed into the ditches.
sentences.idiomatic: The prince withdrew, pressed tens of thousands of market dwellers into service, and marched to the western portal of Weiyang Palace. There he collided with the chancellor's host. For five days the armies slaughtered each other until corpses choked the canals and blood ran in the gutters.

sentences.chinese: 丞相附兵浸多，太子軍敗，南奔覆盎城門，得出。
sentences.literal: The men attached to the chancellor grew ever more numerous; the heir's army was defeated; he fled south to Fugang Gate and got out.
sentences.idiomatic: Reinforcements swelled the government ranks until the crown prince's force broke. He bolted south through Fugang Gate and slipped away.

sentences.chinese: 會夜司直田仁部閉城門，坐令太子得出，丞相欲斬仁。
sentences.literal: It happened that at night the Chief Constable Tian Ren's unit had closed the city gate; he sat by and let the heir escape, and the chancellor wished to execute Ren.
sentences.idiomatic: That night Chief Constable Tian Ren's company had barred the gate, yet he stood aside while the heir fled. The chancellor demanded his head.

sentences.chinese: 御史大夫暴勝之謂丞相曰：「司直，吏二千石，當先請，奈何擅斬之？」
sentences.literal: Imperial Counselor Bao Shengzhi said to the chancellor, "The Chief Constable is an official of two thousand piculs; one should first petition—how dare you execute him on your own?"
sentences.idiomatic: Imperial Counselor Bao Shengzhi protested: "The chief constable holds rank at two thousand piculs. You must seek imperial approval before killing him—what right have you to strike on your own?"

sentences.chinese: 丞相釋仁。
sentences.literal: The chancellor released Ren.
sentences.idiomatic: The chancellor spared Tian Ren.

sentences.chinese: 上聞而大怒，下吏責問御史大夫曰：「司直縱反者，丞相斬之，法也，大夫何以擅止之？」
sentences.literal: When the emperor heard of it he was greatly angered; he cast the matter to the clerks and rebuked the Imperial Counselor: "The Chief Constable let a rebel go free; for the chancellor to behead him is according to law—why did you, sir, presumptuously stop it?"
sentences.idiomatic: When word reached the Son of Heaven, his wrath was terrible. He had the counselors examine Bao Shengzhi: "The chief constable abetted a traitor; the chancellor acted within the law when he sought his life—by what authority did you countermand him?"

sentences.chinese: 勝之皇恐，自殺。
sentences.literal: Shengzhi was terrified and killed himself.
sentences.idiomatic: Bao Shengzhi, panic-stricken, took his own life.

sentences.chinese: 及北軍使者任安，坐受太子節，懷二心，司直田仁縱太子，皆要斬。
sentences.literal: As for Ren An, the commissioner of the Northern Army, he was charged with accepting the heir's staff while harboring divided loyalty; Chief Constable Tian Ren was charged with letting the heir go; both were cut in two at the waist.
sentences.idiomatic: Ren An of the Northern Army, who had taken the prince's tally while his loyalties wavered, and Tian Ren, who had opened the way for the fugitive, were both sentenced to death by waist-slicing.

sentences.chinese: 上曰：「侍郎莽通獲反將如侯，長安男子景通從通獲少傅石德，可謂元功矣。
sentences.literal: The emperor said, "Gentleman for Court Audience Mang Tong captured the rebel general Ruhou; the Chang'an commoner Jing Tong followed Tong and captured Junior Tutor Shi De—they may be called men of the first merit.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor declared: "Mang Tong of the Gentlemen for Court Audience seized the rebel commander Ruhou; the commoner Jing Tong of Chang'an aided him in capturing Junior Tutor Shi De. Both deserve the highest reward.

sentences.chinese: 大鴻臚商丘成力戰獲反將張光。
sentences.literal: Grand Herald Shangqiu Cheng fought hard and captured the rebel general Zhang Guang.
sentences.idiomatic: Grand Herald Shangqiu Cheng fought stubbornly and took rebel commander Zhang Guang alive.

sentences.chinese: 其封通為重合侯，建為德侯，成為秺侯。」
sentences.literal: Therefore enfeoff Tong as Marquis of Chonghe, Jian as Marquis of De, and Cheng as Marquis of Du."
sentences.idiomatic: Enfeoff Mang Tong as Marquis of Chonghe, Jing Tong as Marquis of De, and Shangqiu Cheng as Marquis of Du."

sentences.chinese: 諸太子賓客，嘗出入宮門，皆坐誅。
sentences.literal: All the heir's retainers who had frequented the palace gates were executed for their crime.
sentences.idiomatic: Every client of the crown prince who had passed the palace gates was put to death.

sentences.chinese: 其隨太子發兵，以反法族。
sentences.literal: Those who followed the heir in raising troops were extirpated as rebels under the statutes.
sentences.idiomatic: Anyone who had marched under the prince's banner was extirpated as a traitor.

sentences.chinese: 吏士劫略者，皆徙敦煌郡。
sentences.literal: Soldiers and clerks who had looted were all banished to Dunhuang commandery.
sentences.idiomatic: Soldiers and officials guilty of plunder were transported to Dunhuang.

sentences.chinese: 以太子在外，始置屯兵長安諸城門。
sentences.literal: Because the heir was still at large, garrison troops were first stationed at the various gates of Chang'an.
sentences.idiomatic: With the heir still abroad, the court for the first time posted garrisons on every Chang'an gate.

sentences.chinese: 後二十餘日，太子得於湖。
sentences.literal: More than twenty days later the heir was taken at Hu.
sentences.idiomatic: Some three weeks later the crown prince was run to earth at Hu county.

sentences.chinese: 語在《太子傳》。
sentences.literal: The account is given in the Heir's Biography.
sentences.idiomatic: The particulars are recorded in the crown prince's biography.

sentences.chinese: 其明年，貳師將軍李廣利將兵出擊匈奴，丞相為祖道，送至渭橋，與廣利辭決。
sentences.literal: The next year General-in-chief Li Guangli led troops out to strike the Xiongnu; the chancellor held the farewell rite for him, escorted him to the Wei Bridge, and took leave of Guangli.
sentences.idiomatic: The following year General-in-chief Li Guangli marched against the Xiongnu. The chancellor saw him off with the road sacrifice as far as the Wei Bridge and there clasped hands in farewell.

sentences.chinese: 廣利曰：「願君侯早請昌邑王為太子。
sentences.literal: Guangli said, "I hope, my lord marquis, that you will soon memorialize to make the King of Changyi heir apparent.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Guangli said, "Pray petition early to install the king of Changyi as heir.

sentences.chinese: 如立為帝，君侯長何憂乎？」
sentences.literal: If he is established as emperor, what long-term worry will you, my lord marquis, have?"
sentences.idiomatic: Once he mounts the throne, you will never want for favor, my lord."

sentences.chinese: 屈氂許諾。
sentences.literal: Qu 氂 promised.
sentences.idiomatic: Qu 氂 agreed.

sentences.chinese: 昌邑王者，貳師將軍女弟李夫人子也。
sentences.literal: The King of Changyi was the son of Lady Li, younger sister of the General-in-chief.
sentences.idiomatic: The king of Changyi was the Lady Li's son—Li Guangli's nephew by his sister.

sentences.chinese: 貳師女為屈氂子妻，故共欲立焉。
sentences.literal: The General-in-chief's daughter was wife to Qu 氂's son, so they jointly wished to establish him.
sentences.idiomatic: Because Li Guangli's daughter was married to Qu 氂's son, the two families schemed together to put the youth on the throne.

sentences.chinese: 是時，治巫蠱獄急，內者令郭穰告丞相夫人以丞相數有譴，使巫祠社，祝詛主上，有惡言，及與貳師共禱祠，欲令昌邑王為帝。
sentences.literal: At that time the witchcraft cases were being prosecuted urgently; Chief of Palace Attendants Guo Rang reported that the chancellor's wife, because the chancellor had often been reprimanded, had had witches sacrifice at the altars of soil and grain, cursing the sovereign with evil words, and moreover had joined the General-in-chief in prayer to make the King of Changyi emperor.
sentences.idiomatic: While the witchcraft inquisitions raged, Chief of Palace Attendants Guo Rang denounced the chancellor's wife for hiring witches to curse the emperor at the sheji altars—she was smarting from her husband's repeated humiliations—and for conspiring with Li Guangli to enthrone the king of Changyi.

sentences.chinese: 有司奏請案驗，罪至大逆不道。
sentences.literal: The authorities memorialized requesting investigation; the crime reached to great treason and impiety.
sentences.idiomatic: The ministers asked for a full inquiry; the charges amounted to capital treason.

sentences.chinese: 有詔載屈氂廚車以徇，要斬東市，妻子梟首華陽街。
sentences.literal: There was an edict to load Qu 氂 on a kitchen cart and parade him, to cut him in two at the waist in the eastern market, and to expose his wife's and children's heads on Huayang Street.
sentences.idiomatic: An edict ordered Qu 氂 displayed in the executioner's cart, cut in two at the eastern market, and his wife and children decapitated, their heads spiked along Huayang Street.

sentences.chinese: 貳師將軍妻子亦收。
sentences.literal: The General-in-chief's wife and children were also arrested.
sentences.idiomatic: Li Guangli's family was seized as well.

sentences.chinese: 貳師聞之，降匈奴，宗族遂滅。
sentences.literal: When the General-in-chief heard of it, he surrendered to the Xiongnu, and his clan was then exterminated.
sentences.idiomatic: Hearing the news, Li Guangli defected to the Xiongnu, and his entire lineage was wiped out.

sentences.chinese: 車千秋
sentences.literal: Che Qianqiu.
sentences.idiomatic: Che Qianqiu

sentences.chinese: 車千秋，本姓田氏，其先齊諸田徙長陵。
sentences.literal: Che Qianqiu, originally surnamed Tian, had ancestors among the Tian of Qi who moved to Changling.
sentences.idiomatic: Che Qianqiu was born a Tian; his forebears were Qi princes of the Tian house who had resettled at Changling.

sentences.chinese: 千秋為高寢郎。
sentences.literal: Qianqiu was a gentleman of the chamber for the Lofty Temple.
sentences.idiomatic: He served as gentleman-attendant at the Lofty Temple mausoleum.

sentences.chinese: 會衛太子為江充所譖敗，久之，千秋上急變訟太子冤，曰：「子弄父兵，罪當答；
sentences.literal: It happened that the Wei Heir-Apparent had been slandered by Jiang Chong and ruined; after a long time Qianqiu submitted an urgent memorial pleading the heir's injustice, saying, "A son who borrows his father's weapons deserves the bamboo;
sentences.idiomatic: Long after the Wei crown prince had been destroyed by Jiang Chong's slander, Qianqiu filed an emergency memorial insisting on the prince's innocence: "A son who plays with his father's arms deserves a flogging;

sentences.chinese: 天子之子過誤殺人，當何罷哉！
sentences.literal: for the Son of Heaven's son who kills someone by mistake, what sentence of dismissal applies!"
sentences.idiomatic: but when the emperor's own child takes a life through mischance, what grave penalty could possibly apply?"

sentences.chinese: 臣嘗夢見一白頭翁教臣言。」
sentences.literal: I once dreamed a white-haired old man who taught me these words."
sentences.idiomatic: A white-haired elder spoke these words to me in a dream."

sentences.chinese: 是時，上頗知太子惶恐無他意，乃大感寤，召見千秋。
sentences.literal: At that time the emperor had largely realized that the heir had been frightened and had no other intent, and was greatly moved; he summoned Qianqiu for audience.
sentences.idiomatic: By then the emperor understood that the crown prince had acted only from terror, not treason. Deeply shaken, he summoned Qianqiu.

sentences.chinese: 至前，千秋長八尺餘，體貌甚麗，武帝見而說之，謂曰：「父子之間，人所難言也，公獨明其不然。
sentences.literal: When he came before the throne, Qianqiu was more than eight feet tall, of very handsome bearing; Emperor Wu, pleased at sight of him, said, "Between father and son what men find hard to speak of, you alone have made plain that it was not so.
sentences.idiomatic: Qianqiu stood over eight feet, splendidly proportioned. Emperor Wu took to him at once. "The bond between father and son is what other men dare not parse," he said; "you alone have shown it was never what rumour claimed.

sentences.chinese: 此高廟神靈使公教我，公當遂為吾輔佐。」
sentences.literal: It is the spirits of the Lofty Temple that made you instruct me; you shall forthwith become my helper."
sentences.idiomatic: The spirit of Gaozu sent you to enlighten me; from this day you shall stand at my side."

sentences.chinese: 立拜千秋為大鴻臚。
sentences.literal: He immediately appointed Qianqiu Grand Herald.
sentences.idiomatic: On the spot he named Qianqiu Grand Herald.

sentences.chinese: 數月，遂代劉屈氂為丞相，封富民侯。
sentences.literal: Within several months he then replaced Liu Qu 氂 as chancellor and was enfeoffed Marquis of Fumin.
sentences.idiomatic: A few months later he succeeded Liu Qu 氂 as chancellor and received the marquisate of Fumin.

sentences.chinese: 千秋無他材能術學，又無伐閱功勞，特以一言寤意，旬月取宰相封侯，世未嘗有也。
sentences.literal: Qianqiu had no other talents, learning, or arts, nor battle laurels or accumulated merit; solely because one utterance awakened the ruler's mind, in the space of a month he seized the chancellorship and a marquisate—such a thing had never occurred in the world.
sentences.idiomatic: He possessed neither scholarship nor administrative genius, nor any battlefield glory—yet a single sentence that pierced the emperor's remorse won him the seals of chancellor and a feudal fief within weeks, a rise without parallel in history.

sentences.chinese: 反漢使者至匈奴，單于問曰：「聞漢新拜丞相，何用得之？」
sentences.literal: When a Han envoy reached the Xiongnu, the Chanyu asked, "I hear Han has newly appointed a chancellor—by what merit did he obtain it?"
sentences.idiomatic: When the next Han envoy reached the steppe, the Chanyu asked how the new chancellor had earned his post.

sentences.chinese: 使者曰：「以上書言事故。」
sentences.literal: The envoy said, "Because he submitted a memorial speaking of an affair."
sentences.idiomatic: The envoy answered that he had risen by memorializing on a single incident.

sentences.chinese: 單于曰：「苟如是，漢置丞相，非用賢也，妄一男子上書即得之矣。」
sentences.literal: The Chanyu said, "If it is thus, then when Han sets up a chancellor it is not by employing the worthy—any random fellow who sends up a writing obtains it."
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu snorted: "If that is all it takes, then the Son of Heaven does not choose ministers for virtue—any scribbler who mails a petition may become chancellor."

sentences.chinese: 使者還，道單于語。
sentences.literal: When the envoy returned, he reported the Chanyu's words.
sentences.idiomatic: On his return the envoy repeated the Chanyu's gibe.

sentences.chinese: 武帝以為辱命，欲下之吏。
sentences.literal: Emperor Wu thought it a disgrace to the mission and wished to cast him to the clerks.
sentences.idiomatic: Emperor Wu considered it a humiliation to the court and meant to hand the man to the jailers.

sentences.chinese: 良久，乃貰之。
sentences.literal: After a long while he pardoned him.
sentences.idiomatic: After long reflection he relented and spared him.

sentences.chinese: 然千秋為人敦厚有智，居位自稱，逾於前後數公。
sentences.literal: Yet Qianqiu as a man was honest, thick, and wise; in holding office he acquitted himself, surpassing the several lords who came before and after.
sentences.idiomatic: For all that, Qianqiu was stolid, warm-hearted, and shrewd; in office he conducted himself more creditably than many who had worn the seal before or after.

sentences.chinese: 初，千秋始視事，見上連年治太子獄，誅罰尤多，群下恐懼，思欲寬廣上意，尉安眾庶。
sentences.literal: At first, when Qianqiu first assumed duties, he saw that the emperor year after year prosecuted the heir's cases, executions and punishments being especially many, so that those below were fearful and wished to broaden the emperor's mind and comfort the multitude.
sentences.idiomatic: When he first entered the chancellery he saw how relentlessly the emperor still pursued everyone tied to the crown prince, how the heads piled up, and how terror gripped the bureaucracy. He resolved to soften the sovereign and reassure the people.

sentences.chinese: 乃與御史、中二千石共上壽頌德美，勸上施恩惠，緩刑罰，玩聽音樂，養志和神，為天下自虞樂。
sentences.literal: Thereupon he together with the Imperial Secretary and those ranked at full two thousand piculs jointly offered birthday congratulations, praising virtue and beauty, urging the emperor to extend favor and grace, relax punishments, indulge in listening to music, nourish his will and harmonize his spirit, and for the sake of all under Heaven take his own ease and pleasure.
sentences.idiomatic: He joined the Imperial Secretary and every minister at full two thousand piculs in a court toast, hymning the emperor's virtue and urging him to scatter blessings, lighten sentences, lose himself in music, restore his spirit, and—for the good of the realm—grant himself some joy.

sentences.chinese: 上報曰：「朕之不德，自左丞相與貳師陰謀逆亂，巫蠱之禍流及士大夫。
sentences.literal: The emperor replied, "I lack virtue; from the time the Left Chancellor and the General-in-chief secretly plotted rebellion, the witchcraft calamity flowed to the great officers.
sentences.idiomatic: The throne answered: "I am short of virtue. Ever since the Left Chancellor and Li Guangli conspired in the dark, the witchcraft terror has swallowed half my ministers.

sentences.chinese: 朕日一食者累月，乃何樂之聽？
sentences.literal: I have taken but one meal a day for months—what pleasure is there in listening to music?
sentences.idiomatic: For months I have eaten a single meal a day—what music could I bear to hear?

sentences.chinese: 痛士大夫常在心，既事不咎。
sentences.literal: The pain of the great officers is always in my heart; now that the affair is ended I assign no blame.
sentences.idiomatic: Their suffering haunts me still, yet the matter is closed and I levy no further blame.

sentences.chinese: 雖然，巫蠱始發，詔丞相、御史督二千石求捕，廷尉治，未聞九卿、廷尉有所鞫也。
sentences.literal: Even so, when the witchcraft first broke out, I ordered the chancellor and Imperial Secretary to supervise the two-thousand-piculs officials in seeking arrests, with the Commandant of Justice conducting trials—I have not heard that any of the nine ministers or the Commandant of Justice conducted a genuine examination.
sentences.idiomatic: Nevertheless, when the witchcraft panic first erupted I told chancellor and censor to supervise arrests among the two-thousand-piculs ranks and let the Commandant of Justice try the cases—yet none of the nine ministers or the commandant ever pressed a real interrogation.

sentences.chinese: 曩者，江充先治甘泉宮人，轉至未央椒房，以及敬聲之疇、李禹之屬謀人匈奴，有司無所發，令丞相親掘蘭台蠱驗，所明知也。
sentences.literal: Formerly Jiang Chong first prosecuted the Ganquan Palace people, then shifted to Weiyang and the pepper chambers, reaching to the likes of Jingsheng and the faction of Li Yu plotting entry into the Xiongnu—the authorities uncovered nothing; I ordered the chancellor personally to dig at Orchid Terrace for witchproof—this is what was clearly known.
sentences.idiomatic: Jiang Chong began with the maids of Ganquan, then moved on to Weiyang's harem quarters and clients such as Gongsun Jingsheng or Li Yu's clique, who supposedly plotted to flee to the Xiongnu—yet your bureaus found nothing until I made the chancellor himself unearth straw dolls at Orchid Terrace. You knew the truth all along.

sentences.chinese: 至今余巫頗脫不止，陰賊侵身，遠近為蠱，朕愧之甚，何壽之有？
sentences.literal: Down to the present the remnant witchcraft slips away unchecked; insidious harm invades the body; far and near practice sorcery—I am deeply ashamed—what longevity is there?
sentences.idiomatic: Even now leftover sorcery wriggles free; malign arts gnaw at the court from within and without. I am mortified—what toast to long life can I drink?

sentences.chinese: 敬不舉君之觴！
sentences.literal: Respectfully I do not raise your goblets!
sentences.idiomatic: I cannot in good conscience accept your cups!

sentences.chinese: 謹謝丞相、二千石各就館。
sentences.literal: Let the chancellor and each two-thousand-piculs official return to his lodging.
sentences.idiomatic: Let chancellor and ministers each withdraw to his quarters.

sentences.chinese: 書曰：『毋偏毋黨，王道蕩蕩。』
sentences.literal: The Document says, 'Without partiality, without faction, the kingly way is vast and smooth.'"
sentences.idiomatic: The Classic says: 'Unswayed, uncliqued, the royal way runs level and broad.'"
